message:
chore: suppress clippy too_many_arguments on worker_lifecycle, audit batch 379, beads sync

src/trace/event.rs: adds #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] on the
worker_lifecycle helper function which takes 8 parameters (seq, time,
kind, worker_id, job_id, task, region, obligation). The function is a
private constructor helper called only by the five public worker event
constructors, and splitting it would add boilerplate without improving
clarity.

audit_index.jsonl: adds batch 379 diff audit entries for decoding.rs,
grpc/health.rs, server/shutdown.rs, types/symbol.rs, and
types/symbol_set.rs — all verified SOUND with zero bugs.

.beads/issues.jsonl: synced issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -510,7 +510,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","title":"[AA-07.1] Design attenuated distributed capability token and revocation model","description":"Design the distributed-capability model for Asupersync: attenuated delegation, expiry, caveats, revocation, auditability, and clean interop with the existing local capability boundary.","design":"Scope:\n- Define token structure, attenuation semantics, trust boundaries, and replay resistance.\n- Specify how delegated authority maps back to concrete runtime capabilities.\n- Keep local-only operation as the baseline mode.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Delegated capability semantics are explicit for attenuation, expiry, caveats, and revocation.
2. The design preserves least authority and prevents capability amplification by construction.
3. Threat-model unit scenarios, revocation-drill E2E plans, and audit-log requirements are spelled out in the token model before implementation starts.
4. The design documents when distributed authority is disallowed, what user-visible failure modes should look like, and which structured fields must exist for incident response.","notes":"Primary risk: an elegant token model that weakens the local capability story.\nCountermeasure: make local capability flow the root and force monotonic attenuation.\nFallback: distributed authority stays disabled until proven.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:19.789919212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:50:04.029778993Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T20:50:04.029749348Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","capability","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:10.552847298Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:19.789919212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","title":"[AA-07.2] Prototype controller sandbox membrane and delegated-authority execution path","description":"Implement the execution membrane for adaptive controllers or delegated-authority logic: sandboxed evaluation, explicit capability injection, resource caps, and audited outputs rather than ambient execution.","design":"Scope:\n- Choose the smallest viable sandbox technology and action surface.\n- Enforce time, memory, and capability caps.\n- Keep controller outputs deterministic and easy to inspect.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Sandboxed controller execution cannot escape its granted capability set.\n2. Resource caps and exhaustion behavior are explicit and tested.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover normal execution, cap exhaustion, and attempted capability misuse.\n4. Structured logs record granted capabilities, resource budgets, and sandbox verdicts.","notes":"Primary risk: sandbox adds complexity without real containment.\nCountermeasure: keep the action surface tiny and test adversarial cases.\nFallback: controllers continue to run only in local trusted/static form.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:24.807598623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:16:21.377522397Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:16:21.377497391Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","decision-plane","runtime-ascension","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:15.516015888Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:24.807598623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:13.037146543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","title":"[AA-07.3] Run authority-flow audits, revocation drills, and adversarial validation corpus","description":"Explicit validation bead for the authority track: audit authority flow end-to-end, exercise revocation and expiry drills, and build an adversarial corpus that proves the sandbox and delegation system fails closed.","design":"Scope:\n- Cover confused-deputy, stale-token, over-delegation, replay, and revocation races.\n- Verify evidence and logs are sufficient for incident response.\n- Make failure drills replayable.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E suites cover the major authority abuse scenarios.\n2. Revocation and expiry drills demonstrate safe fail-closed behavior.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts make authority provenance and denial reasons explicit.\n4. Any path that silently expands authority blocks closure.","notes":"Primary risk: validation corpus misses the interesting attack path.\nCountermeasure: keep the corpus explicit, versioned, and easy to grow.\nFallback: disable distributed and controller authority features by default.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:29.850528061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:39:44.749967114Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:39:44.749943470Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa07-authority","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:29.850528061Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:17.996558791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.8","title":"[AA-08] Transport Frontier and Network-Aware Runtime Seams","description":"Explore the transport and networking frontier that could make Asupersync not just an async runtime but a differentiated end-to-end systems substrate: microsecond RPC, multipath, and network-coded delivery under explicit safety and benchmark constraints.","design":"Background:\n- Many runtimes stop at local scheduling; a truly dominant system should own more of the latency path.\n- This track is intentionally experimental and must remain isolated behind sharp seams, replayable traces, and conservative go/no-go gates.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Feasibility/benchmark harness for receiver-driven low-latency RPC.\n- Multipath + FEC/RLNC integration path for network-aware execution.\n- Decision/fallback contract for transport-level adaptivity.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Transport-frontier work is isolated behind explicit seams and benchmark harnesses before any invasive adoption decision.
2. Multipath/FEC ideas are tied to replayable traces, not anecdotal wins.
3. Network-aware controller behavior has explicit fallback to conservative transport modes.
4. No experimental transport mechanism can merge without deterministic validation and clear interaction boundaries with the runtime core.
5. The track provides deterministic unit and E2E network-replay coverage, structured transport diagnostics, and explicit downgrade commands so users can tell exactly when and why the runtime stays on the conservative path.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: open a path toward transport-aware async execution.\n- EV: medium-to-high strategic upside; lower immediate certainty than core scheduler/static-safety tracks.\n- Primary risk: broad scope and interaction complexity.\n- Countermeasure: feasibility-first, benchmark-first, and explicit dependency on mature FEC/correctness work where required.\n- Conservative fallback: existing networking/runtime seams remain the default product surface.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"RusticBarn","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:50.530144558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T21:32:03.857190273Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","networking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:50.530144558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:18.107322294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Transport-frontier work is isolated behind explicit seams and benchmark harnesses before any invasive adoption decision.
2. Multipath/FEC ideas are tied to replayable traces, not anecdotal wins.
3. Network-aware controller behavior has explicit fallback to conservative transport modes.
4. No experimental transport mechanism can merge without deterministic validation and clear interaction boundaries with the runtime core.
5. The track provides deterministic unit and E2E network-replay coverage, structured transport diagnostics, and explicit downgrade commands so users can tell exactly when and why the runtime stays on the conservative path.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: open a path toward transport-aware async execution.\n- EV: medium-to-high strategic upside; lower immediate certainty than core scheduler/static-safety tracks.\n- Primary risk: broad scope and interaction complexity.\n- Countermeasure: feasibility-first, benchmark-first, and explicit dependency on mature FEC/correctness work where required.\n- Conservative fallback: existing networking/runtime seams remain the default product surface.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"CyanOwl","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:50.530144558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:12:02.564473678Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","networking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:50.530144558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:18.107322294Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-08.1] Build transport-frontier feasibility harness and benchmark contract","description":"Create the feasibility harness, workload corpus, and benchmark contract for transport-frontier experiments such as receiver-driven low-latency RPC, multipath, and coded transport.","design":"Scope:\n- Define transport-focused workloads and traces.\n- Capture the metrics that matter: RTT tails, grant/path queueing, HOL blocking, fairness, goodput, CPU per packet, and failure handling.\n- Keep the track explicitly exploratory until the harness exists.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Transport experiments share a deterministic workload and replay vocabulary.\n2. Benchmark contract names tail, fairness, goodput, and CPU metrics before prototype work begins.\n3. Workloads include burst loss, reordering, handoff, and overload regimes.\n4. Structured logs and artifacts are replayable and comparable across candidate designs.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.8.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.8.4.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:44.800374371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:33.429860737Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:33.429751283Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","benchmarking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:44.800374371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-08.2] Prototype multipath/FEC/RLNC runtime seam behind explicit experimental gate","description":"Prototype the narrow runtime seam needed for multipath and coded transport decisions, explicitly gated on mature FEC correctness and kept behind a non-default experimental path.","design":"Scope:\n- Reuse existing RaptorQ maturity work instead of bypassing it.\n- Keep transport decisions pluggable and reversible.\n- Avoid contaminating the default runtime path with speculative logic.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Prototype surfaces are isolated behind explicit experimental gates and documented non-goals.\n2. The bead depends on existing RaptorQ correctness closure for any FEC-backed path.\n3. Unit tests cover path selection/coding decisions and conservative fallback behavior.\n4. Structured logs record path/coding policy IDs, fallback reasons, and workload IDs.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.8.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.8.5.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:50.065531405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:38.111237073Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:38.111125385Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","networking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:50.065531405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8.3","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-08.3] Validate network replay, fallback, fairness, and tail behavior for transport experiments","description":"Explicit validation bead for the transport track: replay burst-loss/handoff/overload scenarios, verify conservative fallback, and measure whether any experimental transport seam actually improves the targeted metrics.","design":"Scope:\n- Run network replay rather than one-shot local benchmarks.\n- Include fairness, HOL blocking, tail latency, and CPU cost.\n- Verify that fallback returns the system to conservative transport behavior without semantic surprises.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic replay tests cover handoff, burst loss, overload, and fallback entry/exit.\n2. Comparator tables report p50/p95/p99, fairness, CPU, and goodput against the conservative transport baseline.\n3. Structured logs capture path decisions, coding decisions, fallback causes, and replay metadata.\n4. No experimental transport path can graduate without clear wins on the benchmark contract.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.8.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.8.6.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:55.042821413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:42.838366657Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:42.838243889Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","testing","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:50:55.042821413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -913,7 +913,7 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1ky0b","title":"[AA][Formal] Cancellation protocol formalization","description":"## Goal
Formalize the cancellation protocol as a state machine with budgets and phase transitions.

## Why
Cancellation is a multi‑phase protocol (request→drain→finalize). We need proof‑level clarity.

## Deliverables
- Formal state machine spec with guards and budgets.
- Proof sketch of idempotence and bounded cleanup.
- Mapping from runtime transitions to spec.

## Acceptance
- Spec covers CancelRequested/Cancelling/Finalizing/Completed.
- Proves that all tasks eventually reach terminal outcomes under sufficient budgets.

## Dependencies
- Uses the core small‑step semantics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:05:36.612171824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:39.817956116Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:39.817887909Z","close_reason":"Formalized cancellation protocol with budgets, idempotence, bounded cleanup","external_ref":"bd-1b5t","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"asupersync-1ky3w","title":"[WASM][QA+DX] Evidence, examples, troubleshooting, and release gates for vNext surfaces","description":"Extend the Browser Edition quality spine, examples, troubleshooting, and release gates so every new feasible vNext surface ships with artifact-backed evidence. The original Browser Edition quality work was broad, but it predates the specific vNext surfaces in this new branch.\n\nThis epic ensures the new direct-worker, durable-storage, host-capability, Rust-authored, and optional-offload lanes are all held to the same standard as the original Browser Edition rollout.","design":"Treat verification and DX as first-class product work. Every newly supported surface should have unit evidence, packaged consumer or browser-run evidence where appropriate, troubleshooting guidance, and release/promotion gates that fail closed on drift.","acceptance_criteria":"1. The evidence matrix and CI lanes cover every new vNext surface added by this branch.\n2. Maintained examples and fixtures exercise the new surfaces rather than leaving them as undocumented expert-only paths.\n3. Troubleshooting and release/promotion materials explicitly distinguish supported, guarded, bridge-only, and impossible surfaces.","notes":"Do not let new capabilities land as code-only wins. If users and future maintainers cannot learn, validate, debug, and release them deterministically, the capability is not actually finished.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:16:30.980912262Z","created_by":"WindyStream","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:16:39.764528574Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","dx","qa","release","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","depends_on_id":"asupersync-g6uho","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:16:39.764467320Z","created_by":"WindyStream","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","depends_on_id":"asupersync-n6kwt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:16:36.772342531Z","created_by":"WindyStream","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","title":"[WASM][QA+DX] Extend the evidence matrix and CI lanes for every new browser vNext surface","description":"Expand the Browser Edition evidence and CI story so every new vNext surface is covered by an explicit lane instead of relying on ad hoc local validation. This includes direct-worker support, durable storage, host-capability expansion, the Rust-authored browser path, and optional offload or guarded parallel execution where applicable.

The purpose is to keep the entire new branch releaseable and reviewable as one coherent product surface.","design":"Treat evidence coverage as a matrix of browser surfaces and verification modes rather than as one monolithic test lane. Each new direct, guarded, or bridge-aware surface should have the right combination of unit, browser-run, contract, and release-blocking evidence.

CI should fail closed when a surface loses its corresponding evidence lane or when the documented support matrix drifts away from the checks.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every new vNext browser surface has explicit CI or release-lane coverage appropriate to its risk and support level.
2. The evidence matrix is documented and maintained rather than inferred from job names.
3. Regressions in worker, storage, host, Rust, or optional-parallel surfaces become mechanically visible in CI.","notes":"This task is intentionally cross-cutting. It is how the branch becomes maintainable by future selves and future agents instead of depending on memory and manual retesting.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BlackWaterfall","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:34:51.803151406Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:01:00.141934777Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","ci","qa","release","testing","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-18tbo.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:35:12.314947454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:51:19.666113085Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1n453.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:48.611983448Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2jhnk.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:54.365477894Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3ak5y.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:45.709200738Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4l9iw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:51.432412492Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","title":"[WASM][QA+DX] Add maintained fixtures and end-user examples for worker, storage, and Rust browser lanes","description":"Create the maintained fixtures and end-user-facing examples that make the new browser surfaces approachable instead of expert-only. This task focuses on the browser lanes where examples matter most to adoption and debugging: direct worker execution, durable storage and artifact flows, and the public Rust-authored browser path.

Its job is to ensure the new functionality can be learned and validated without reading deep implementation internals.","design":"Curate examples and fixtures that match how real users will approach the Browser Edition: worker-based execution, durable browser storage, artifact export, and Rust-authored browser code. The goal is not example sprawl; it is a maintained set of representative flows that future maintainers can use as both teaching material and regression evidence.

Examples should reflect the actual support matrix and should make capability boundaries visible rather than hiding them behind convenience magic.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Maintained examples or fixtures exist for direct worker execution, durable storage flows, and the public Rust-authored browser lane.
2. The examples are simple enough to teach from but realistic enough to catch regressions in the intended user paths.
3. The example set aligns with the supported browser matrix rather than demonstrating unsupported shortcuts.","notes":"Examples are part of the Browser Edition product, not decoration. They are the fastest way for future selves to understand whether the roadmap work became usable.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:47:56.837122005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:14.386419421Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","dx","example","qa","testing","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-18tbo.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:00.856758899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:47:56.837122005Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3ak5y.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:05.440218550Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4l9iw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:14.386339874Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","title":"[WASM][QA+DX] Add maintained fixtures and end-user examples for worker, storage, and Rust browser lanes","description":"Create the maintained fixtures and end-user-facing examples that make the new browser surfaces approachable instead of expert-only. This task focuses on the browser lanes where examples matter most to adoption and debugging: direct worker execution, durable storage and artifact flows, and the public Rust-authored browser path.

3. The example set aligns with the supported browser matrix rather than demonstrating unsupported shortcuts.","notes":"Examples are part of the Browser Edition product, not decoration. They are the fastest way for future selves to understand whether the roadmap work became usable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageCrane","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:47:56.837122005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:11:44.229319150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","dx","example","qa","testing","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-18tbo.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:00.856758899Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:47:56.837122005Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-3ak5y.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:05.440218550Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-4l9iw.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:14.386339874Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1ky3w.3","title":"[WASM][QA+DX] Expand troubleshooting and support guidance across direct, guarded, bridge-only, and impossible browser surfaces","description":"Publish the support and troubleshooting material that ties the whole wasm browser roadmap together. This task explains how users and future maintainers should think about the direct browser runtime, guarded optional lanes, bridge-only integrations, and the surfaces that are simply impossible in modern browsers.

It exists so support guidance grows out of the same matrix as the implementation work instead of becoming a separate folklore document.","design":"Write troubleshooting and support guidance from the four-bucket model established in the boundary epic: direct, guarded, bridge-only, and impossible. The material should help users diagnose why a given environment or feature path is or is not supported without forcing them to reverse-engineer implementation details.

This should become the stable language layer that keeps future browser additions consistent.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Support guidance explains the difference between direct browser support, guarded optional lanes, bridge-only integrations, and impossible surfaces.
2. Common failure and confusion cases have actionable troubleshooting guidance tied to the actual support matrix.
3. The support language stays aligned with diagnostics, tests, and docs rather than becoming a parallel truth.","notes":"This task matters because users do not experience the architecture diagram; they experience support outcomes. The guide should let them understand those outcomes quickly and correctly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:05.724590780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:23.542852801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","docs","dx","qa","support","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:05.724590780Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:23.542789664Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-g6uho.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:18.867479037Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1ky3w.4","title":"[WASM][QA+DX] Add promotion, rollback, and release-gate policy for new browser vNext surfaces","description":"Define how new browser surfaces graduate, how they are held behind gates while immature, and how they should be rolled back or downgraded if they regress. This task turns the vNext browser roadmap into something that can be operated safely in releases rather than only implemented once.

Its job is to preserve quality and honesty when the project ships new browser support incrementally.","design":"Define explicit graduation and rollback rules for the new browser surfaces so releases remain honest as support expands. The policy should describe what evidence is required before a surface becomes fully supported, when a surface should stay guarded or preview-only, and how maintainers should respond if a release proves a surface unstable.

This task closes the branch by making quality decisions operational rather than ad hoc.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Promotion criteria exist for new direct or guarded browser surfaces.
2. Rollback or downgrade policy exists for surfaces that regress after shipping.
3. Release gates tie back to evidence and support-matrix truth rather than optimistic intention.","notes":"This is the release-operations counterpart to the technical work. Without it, the roadmap could still devolve into unclear support promises even if the code is good.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageCrane","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:11.042653470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-16T04:06:22Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["browser","qa","release","rollback","vnext","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:48:11.042653470Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:29.276984562Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1ky3w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T06:49:33.784948764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
